surface.name: Image and Video Developer Kickstart through a completed SDK quick start
surface.selection_basis: The Image and Video documentation presents the hands-on Developer Kickstart as an interactive onboarding route. Its final required task is to pick a supported SDK quick start and successfully run that project, but it does not select a language, runtime, or backend, frontend, or mobile route for the developer.

alternatives_considered.name: SDK quick starts index
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The index is also recommended for onboarding but immediately requires a choice among backend, frontend, and mobile SDKs; the Developer Kickstart provides the broader official common spine before the same choice.

alternatives_considered.name: Developer Kickstart with Python SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Python is a peer language option and the official route does not rank it above Node.js or the other SDKs.

alternatives_considered.name: Google or GitHub registration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are peer account-creation branches; the documented email route was used for the reconstructable candidate because Cloudinary also documents its email-verification requirement.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Cloudinary account, find product-environment credentials, complete the Image and Video Developer Kickstart, and run one official SDK quick start through its demonstrated media-delivery terminal.
assumed_prior_state: No Cloudinary account, product environment credentials, SDK, sample project, uploaded asset, or delivered transformation is assumed.
The developer can receive account email, can accept Cloudinary terms, and has a local development environment for whichever SDK route they choose.
No registration submission, email verification, credential retrieval, upload, API request, or asset delivery was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: I successfully ran my first SDK quick start project!
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Run a selected official SDK quick start end to end and observe its documented media output.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: For the fully reconstructed Node.js candidate, the script logs configuration, upload and resource responses, prints an image tag, and the transformed image appears when its URL is opened in a browser.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: I successfully ran my first SDK quick start project!

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Developer Kickstart uses successful execution of a selected SDK quick start as its final required progress marker. The Node.js candidate demonstrates the corresponding end-to-end output as a transformed image loaded from the generated URL.

prerequisites.requirement: A free Cloudinary account created with Google, GitHub, or an email address; the email route requires clicking the emailed verification link before the account can be used.
Accept Cloudinary's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy when creating the account.
Cloud name, API key, API secret, and API environment-variable format from Console Settings > API Keys; keep the API secret out of client code and version control.
A supported development environment for the chosen official SDK quick start; the reconstructed candidate requires Node.js and npm.

primary_path.action: Open the Developer Kickstart and begin its ordered onboarding tasks.
details: The route is presented as a collection of quick tasks covering optimization, transformation, asset management, and an SDK project.
primary_path.output: The Kickstart progress sequence from Intro through Step 5 is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page displays the progress control 'Got it - I'm ready to get started'.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No navigation recovery behavior is documented.

primary_path.action: Register for a free Cloudinary account using Google, GitHub, or email.
details: The email form asks for full name, email, and a password with the displayed composition rules.
Submitting the email form accepts the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
primary_path.input: A selected registration method and its required identity information
primary_path.success_signal: For email registration, Cloudinary sends a verification email; the account becomes usable only after its link is opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The registration surface documents country restrictions, existing organization-managed accounts, invitations, and a generic retry or alternate-method error.

primary_path.action: Open Console Settings, navigate to API Keys, and locate the product environment cloud name, API key, API secret, and API environment variable.
details: Optionally customize a new product environment's cloud name before it is used in production; changing an in-use cloud name can break delivered asset URLs.
primary_path.output: Product-environment credentials are available for the SDK route.
primary_path.success_signal: The Kickstart progress markers for having an account and knowing where to find credentials can be checked.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The API secret may require password verification before it can be viewed or copied.

primary_path.action: Complete the Kickstart's ordered optimization, first-transformation, and resize tasks, then continue to Step 5.
details: Step 2 contains four browser tasks, Step 3 contains four transformation tasks, and Step 4 contains two resize tasks; the complete Node.js candidate below preserves those actions in order.
primary_path.input: The account cloud name and the Kickstart sample delivery URLs
primary_path.output: A prepared developer reaches the final SDK-project task.
primary_path.success_signal: The Step 5 page presents backend, frontend, and mobile quick-start groups.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing URL slash produces an error in Step 2; the guide says to inspect the URL and restore the missing slash. The generative-fill resize may take a few seconds on its first request.

primary_path.action: Choose a backend, frontend, or mobile SDK and a supported language or framework, then perform that official quick start.
details: Backend routes include Node.js, Python, PHP, Java, Ruby/Rails, .NET, Go, and Dart.
Frontend and mobile routes have different setup and completion sequences.
primary_path.input: The developer's intended runtime and application surface
primary_path.success_signal: No single SDK path is selected by the official guide.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The route cannot converge without a human implementation choice.

candidate_paths.name: Email registration and Node.js backend SDK quick start
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This is one complete official peer route. Email registration has a documented verification requirement, and Node.js is the first backend SDK listed, but neither is asserted to be the universal default.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates the registration and SDK choices in the common path, completes every required browser task in Kickstart Steps 2 through 4, and continues through the Node.js quick start's demonstrated transformed-image terminal.

steps.action: Choose Sign Up with Email, enter full name, email, and a compliant password, then select Get Started Now.
details: The password requires at least eight characters, lowercase, uppercase, number, special character, and no spaces.
Selecting the button accepts the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
steps.input: Full name, email, and compliant password
steps.success_signal: The official help article says the verification email is sent automatically.
steps.failure_or_wait: If the email is missing, request a new verification email or password-reset email.

steps.action: Open the verification link from the Cloudinary email to complete signup and activate the account.
steps.success_signal: The account can use Cloudinary APIs and tools after verification.
steps.failure_or_wait: Email link rewriting can interfere; Cloudinary says to copy and paste the original link directly if needed.

steps.action: Open Console Settings > API Keys and copy the API environment-variable format, replacing its API-key and API-secret placeholders with the displayed values.
details: The cloud name is already included in the format.
Do not expose the API secret or commit it to version control.
steps.success_signal: Cloud name, API key, API secret, and environment-variable format are visible.
steps.failure_or_wait: Viewing the secret can require password verification.

steps.action: Open the Step 2 sample image delivery URL with the account cloud name and inspect the original image.
details: The guide describes the starting sample as an approximately 800 KB, 1870 by 1250 JPG included with the account.
steps.input: The Step 2 image URL with `<your_cloud_name>` replaced
steps.success_signal: The high-resolution JPG is visible in the browser.
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for the initial image request.

steps.action: Insert `/q_auto/f_auto` after `/upload`, reload the image, inspect its size and format, and compare it with the original.
steps.output: An automatically optimized image variant loads.
steps.success_signal: The guide says inspection shows roughly one third or less of the original file size without an obvious visual-quality difference.
steps.failure_or_wait: If a URL component is missing a slash, inspect the URL and restore the slash before reloading.

steps.action: Insert `/c_scale,w_500` before `/q_auto/f_auto`, reload the image, and inspect the delivered size and format.
steps.output: A 500-pixel scaled and optimized image loads.
steps.success_signal: The resized delivery is visible and its file size and format can be inspected.
steps.failure_or_wait: If a URL component is missing a slash, inspect the URL and restore the slash before reloading.

steps.action: Open the sample video with the account cloud name, inspect the original, add the same scale and automatic optimization parameters, reload it, then let it play to the end before checking the result.
details: The guide requires waiting until playback ends so progressive optimization can complete.
steps.output: The optimized video variant completes playback.
steps.success_signal: The developer can compare the optimized video's format and file size with the original.
steps.failure_or_wait: The required playback wait is documented; no separate recovery behavior is stated.

steps.action: Add `/e_grayscale` before `/c_scale,w_500` and reload the Step 3 image URL.
steps.input: The optimized and resized image URL
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for this task.

steps.action: Add `/bo_40px_solid_green` after `/e_grayscale` and reload the URL.
steps.output: The black-and-white image loads with a solid green border.
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for this task.

steps.action: Add the published Good morning text-layer and layer-apply components after the border, remove `e_grayscale`, and reload the URL.
details: The text is orange, bottom-right aligned, and offset by 60 pixels in each direction.
steps.output: The color image loads with the text layer.
steps.success_signal: The border and Good morning text are visible on the color image.
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for this task.

steps.action: Remove `/f_auto`, change the extension from `.jpg` to `.png`, reload and inspect the PNG, then restore `/f_auto` and reload again.
steps.output: A forced PNG and then an automatically selected delivery format load in sequence.
steps.success_signal: Inspection first shows PNG; after restoring `f_auto`, Cloudinary selects a browser-appropriate format.
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for this task.

steps.action: Replace `c_scale,w_500` with each documented automatic crop or pad transformation, then keep the predominant-gradient variant and test it against each listed sample public ID.
details: The required variants include automatic banner and portrait crops, orange padding, generative fill, and predominant-gradient padding.
steps.output: Each crop or pad variant loads across the listed sample assets.
steps.success_signal: The developer sees the impact of the different automatic resize transformations.
steps.failure_or_wait: The first generative-fill request may take a few seconds; no other recovery behavior is documented.

steps.action: Return to the simple scale-cropping URL, replace its resize component with each documented face-detection crop, and inspect both results.
details: The guide uses `g_faces` with a portrait fill and with a rounded square automatic crop.
steps.success_signal: The developer sees how face-detection gravity preserves people during resizing.
steps.failure_or_wait: No recovery behavior is documented for this task.

steps.action: In a supported Node.js environment, run `npm install cloudinary`.
steps.failure_or_wait: No installation recovery behavior is documented in the quick start.

steps.action: Set the CLOUDINARY_URL environment variable for the current platform using the copied credential values.
details: Use the documented export command on macOS or Linux or set command on Windows.
steps.output: The SDK can read product-environment credentials.
steps.success_signal: The later script logs the expected cloud_name, api_key, api_secret, and secure configuration.
steps.failure_or_wait: An incorrect value is exposed by a mismatching or failed configuration output.

steps.action: Create index.js, require Cloudinary v2, enable secure URLs, and add the published uploadImage, getAssetInfo, and createImageTag functions.
details: The upload uses the happy_people.jpg remote URL, filename-derived public ID, overwrite, and no random suffix.
The resource call requests detected colors.
The transformation creates a 250 by 250 circular face thumbnail with outline and background colors.
steps.output: A complete Node.js script ready to execute.
steps.success_signal: The main function uploads the sample, retrieves its colors, creates an image tag, and logs it.
steps.failure_or_wait: The sample catches and prints upload or resource errors but does not document a recovery sequence.

steps.output: Configuration, upload response, resource response, and generated image tag are printed.
steps.success_signal: The upload response includes public_id and secure_url, the resource response includes colors, and the generated tag contains the transformation URL.
steps.failure_or_wait: Errors are logged by the sample; no retry sequence is documented.

steps.action: Open the transformed URL from the generated image tag in a browser.
steps.output: The transformed happy_people image is delivered.
steps.success_signal: The browser displays the circular face thumbnail with the documented outline and background transformations.
steps.failure_or_wait: No delivery retry behavior is documented in the quick start.

branches.path: Continue through the selected provider's authentication and consent flow instead of the email form and email-verification sequence.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the account-creation actions while converging on a Cloudinary account.

branches.path: In Product Environments, edit the environment, choose a unique cloud name, and save before using delivered asset URLs.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a recommended configuration step and changes the base identifier in delivered URLs.

branches.condition: Choose another SDK surface or runtime
branches.path: Follow the corresponding backend, frontend, or mobile quick start rather than the Node.js candidate.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation, configuration, code, execution, and terminal details.

friction_gates.description: A Cloudinary account is required for the Kickstart and SDK route.
Email registration requires clicking a verification link before APIs and tools can be used.
The backend SDK route requires a product-environment cloud name, API key, and API secret, with the secret protected from exposure.
The official final Kickstart task requires a human choice among materially different backend, frontend, and mobile SDK paths.

time_to_first_success.value: about 25 minutes for the Developer Kickstart; 5 minutes or less for an SDK quick start

sources.title: Step 1: Register and find credentials
sections_used: Task 1: If you don't yet have a Cloudinary account, register for a free one
Task 2: Find your product environment credentials in your console
Task 3: Optional. Customize your cloud name

sources.title: Why am I receiving an "Upload failed: cloud_name is disabled" error when using my new Cloudinary account?
Where do I find my API key and API secret credentials?
sections_used: Where do I find my API key and API secret credentials?

sources.title: Node.js quick start
sections_used: 1. Set up and configure the SDK
3. Get and use details of the image

sources.title: Step 2: Load pages faster with optimization
sections_used: Task 1: Open the image delivery URL

sources.title: Step 3: Your first transformations
sections_used: Task 1: Add a grayscale effect to your image
Task 2: Add a border to your black and white image
evidence_supported: Effect, border, text-layer, and format outputs

sources.title: Step 4: Resize transformations
sections_used: Task 1: Apply different automatic resize and crop options
Task 2: Try out some face-detection cropping options

sources.title: Step 5: Set up and run your first SDK project
sections_used: Task 1: Try an SDK Quick Start
Task 2: Optional. Take a look at our community libraries

uncertainties.question: Which backend, frontend, or mobile SDK and language should represent Cloudinary's universal first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The installation, credential use, code, terminal artifact, and exact step count differ by selection.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Developer Kickstart says to pick a favorite language and presents the SDKs as peer routes without choosing one.

excluded_after_success: Production hardening, secret management beyond the quick-start warning, additional uploads, asset organization, add-ons, advanced transformations, video workflows, DAM, MediaFlows, and production rollout occur after the documented first SDK project.
The optional community-library exploration in Developer Kickstart Step 5 is after the required successful SDK quick-start marker.
